text: The increasing popularity of detection proposals in high-performance object detection algorithms means that the previous CPU-intensive full sliding window search is no longer needed. As these proposal methods are used in many detection frameworks, their trade-offs are not well-studied. We systematically evaluate twelve different proposal methods, along with four sliding window baselines, for repeatability and recall on the popular PASCAL, ImageNet and MS COCO datasets. In addition, we analyze the impact of proposal methods on state-of-the-art object detection algorithms such as DPM, R-CNN and Fast R-CNN. Our study shows that there is a strong trade-off between accurate localization and high recall. To quantify the relationship between the two, we introduce Average Recall (AR), which measures the ratio of true positive instances that have high overlap with the ground-truth, and has strong correlation with the detection performance of different algorithms. Our study sheds light on the strengths and limitations of the various proposal methods, and helps in choosing optimal proposal methods and parameters for high performance object detection algorithms.
explanation: The passage reads like a technical abstract with precise terminology and standard datasets/methods (PASCAL, ImageNet, MS COCO, DPM, R-CNN, Fast R-CNN). This makes it plausible as human-authored text, though such content can also be AI-generated, so the classification is ambiguous.
